messages.content: So `Subquery` inherits from `BaseExpression` (not `Expression`), and `Exists` inherits from `Subquery`. That's why they don't have `deconstruct` methods - they don't inherit from `Expression` which has `@deconstructible`.¥n¥nNow, the PR description says to apply the same technique to other expressions. The key insight is:¥n¥n1. Classes that have `deconstruct` (inherited from `Expression` via `@deconstructible`) currently deconstruct to `django.db.models.expressions.ClassName`¥n2. Since they're importable from `django.db.models`, they should deconstruct to `django.db.models.ClassName`¥n¥nThe approach would be to add `@deconstructible(path='django.db.models.ClassName')` to each class that:¥n1. Is importable from `django.db.models`¥n2. Currently inherits `deconstruct` from `Expression`¥n¥nBut wait - there's a subtlety. The `@deconstructible` decorator adds `__new__` and `deconstruct` methods. If we add it to subclasses, it would override the inherited ones. Let me check if that's an issue.¥n¥nActually, looking at the `deconstructible` code more carefully:¥n¥n```python¥ndef deconstruct(obj):¥n    if path and type(obj) is klass:¥n        module_name, _, name = path.rpartition('.')¥n    else:¥n        module_name = obj.__module__¥n        name = obj.__class__.__name__¥n    ...¥n    return (¥n        path¥n        if path and type(obj) is klass¥n        else f'{obj.__class__.__module__}.{name}',¥n        obj._constructor_args[0],¥n        obj._constructor_args[1],¥n    )¥n```¥n¥nThe `type(obj) is klass` check ensures that the path is only used when the object is exactly of that class, not a subclass. So if we add `@deconstructible(path='django.db.models.Func')` to `Func`, then `Func()` would deconstruct to `django.db.models.Func`, but a subclass of `Func` would still use its own module path.¥n¥nBut there's a problem: `@deconstructible` also adds `__new__` which captures constructor args. If we add it to subclasses, it would override the parent's `__new__`. Let me check if this is compatible.¥n¥nActually, looking at the code, `__new__` just captures `_constructor_args` and calls `super().__new__`. So adding it to subclasses should be fine - it would just capture the constructor args for that specific class.¥n¥nLet me look at what classes we should add paths for. The classes exported from `django.db.models` that have `deconstruct` are:¥n- Case, Expression, ExpressionList, ExpressionWrapper, Func, OrderBy, OuterRef, RowRange, Value, ValueRange, When, Window, WindowFrame¥n¥nAnd `F` already has it.¥n¥nBut wait - `Expression` is the base class. Should we add a path for `Expression` too? Let me check what the current behavior is:
